metadata.title: GRC Pipeline Challenge — Compliant GCS Storage Pipeline
metadata.remarks: Component definition for the six-week GRC Engineering Club pipeline challenge, implemented on GCP. Every implemented requirement names the actual Terraform resource or Rego policy that implements it and links to a Cosign-signed evidence bundle in this repository. Controls implemented in code but not covered by a signed evidence bundle (AU-3 access logging from Week 1) are deliberately NOT claimed here — a claim without traceable signed evidence is not a claim an auditor can use.

components.title: Compliant GCS storage pipeline (weeks 1-5)
components.description: Terraform-managed Google Cloud Storage with encryption, access enforcement, configuration guardrails, and Data Access audit logging; policy-as-code enforcement via OPA/conftest in CI; evidence chain of custody via Cosign keyless signing.

control-implementations.description: NIST SP 800-53 Revision 5 controls implemented by the pipeline, each traced to its Terraform resource or Rego policy and to a signed evidence bundle.

implemented-requirements.description: Protection of information at rest: google_storage_bucket.primary (week-1/main.tf) uses Google-managed encryption at rest; the OPA policy sc28_encryption_gcp.rego (week-2/policies) fails the CI gate for any planned bucket that disables it. The linked CI-signed bundle contains the conftest evaluation proving the policy ran against the live Terraform plan.

links.text: CI-signed conftest evaluation (Cosign keyless, GitHub Actions OIDC)

implemented-requirements.description: Access enforcement: google_storage_bucket.primary (week-1/main.tf) sets uniform_bucket_level_access = true and public_access_prevention = enforced; ac3_no_public_gcp.rego (week-2/policies) fails the CI gate for any plan that weakens either. The linked CI-signed bundle contains the conftest evaluation.

links.text: CI-signed conftest evaluation (Cosign keyless, GitHub Actions OIDC)

implemented-requirements.description: Configuration settings, mapped by clause: (a) establish/document — Terraform declares the settings (week-1 labels, week-5 org policies); (b) implement — applied and live-verified; (d) monitor/control changes — cm6_required_labels_gcp.rego fails the CI gate on drift (CI-signed bundle) and the org-policy plan is in the Week 5 signed bundle. Clause (c) — deviation identification and approval — is NOT evidenced: no exception register or approval workflow exists, so CM-6 is partially satisfied, for in-scope components only (no org-wide benchmark baseline such as CIS is adopted).

links.text: Week 5 signed bundle (org-policy plan, Cosign keyless, Google OIDC)

implemented-requirements.description: Event logging: google_project_iam_audit_config.storage and .iam (week-5/main.tf) enable Data Access audit logs (DATA_READ/DATA_WRITE for storage.googleapis.com, DATA_READ for iam.googleapis.com), scoped to named services rather than allServices. A live storage.objects.list Data Access entry was captured post-apply. The linked Week 5 bundle contains the applied plan and capture evidence.

links.text: Week 5 signed bundle (Cosign keyless, Google OIDC)

implemented-requirements.description: Non-repudiation, scoped to EVIDENCE PRODUCTION: evidence bundles are Cosign keyless-signed with the signature recorded in the Rekor public transparency log, so neither the pipeline operator nor the CI system can later deny producing a given bundle; week-4/verify-evidence.sh proves the chain (CHAIN INTACT) against a pinned signer identity. Data Access audit logs (week-5/main.tf) additionally bind identity to bucket operations, but this claim does NOT extend to non-repudiation of arbitrary user actions in the environment, and audit records are signed at bundling time, not at emission — the window between emission and bundling is unattested. AU-10 is not in the 800-53B Low/Moderate baselines; this is a beyond-baseline implementation.

links.text: Week 5 signed bundle (Cosign keyless, Google OIDC, Rekor logIndex 2211089201)

implemented-requirements.description: Audit record generation: google_project_iam_audit_config resources (week-5/main.tf) turn on Data Access record generation at the project for the two services the pipeline actually uses; generation was verified live (storage.objects.list entry in the _Default data_access log). The linked Week 5 bundle contains the applied plan. Known seam: AU-12 generates records with the content AU-3 defines, and AU-3 is excluded from this profile for lack of signed evidence — AU-12's content requirement is therefore asserted (Cloud Audit Logs' fixed schema), not independently evidenced.

links.text: Week 5 signed bundle (Cosign keyless, Google OIDC)
